title: paeonia foemina subsp. humilis
intro: Paeonia foemina subsp. humilis, also known as the low-growing peony or woodland peony, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Paeoniaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of North America and is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic agent. The plant has been used to treat a range of health issues, including menstrual cramps, fever, and rheumatism, and has been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Paeonia foemina subsp. humilis may include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support menstrual health, reduce pain and inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
